title: Guzmania straminea
intro: Guzmania straminea, also known as the Stramineous Guzmania, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania straminea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tea, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Guzmania straminea is classified within the family Bromeliaceae. This family consists of over 57 genera and approximately 3,000 species of plants, commonly known as bromeliads. The genus Guzmania, to which Guzmania straminea belongs, comprises around 120 species of epiphytic plants, mostly native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Guzmania straminea, in particular, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, growing in a variety of habitats, including cloud forests and subtropical regions. Guzmania straminea is further classified within the tribe Tillandsioideae, which includes a diverse range of bromeliads with distinct characteristics and adaptations.
